title: LLMs are no longer wholly reliant on the internet
description: Why modern language-model quality still starts with internet-scale pretraining but increasingly depends on mid-training, post-training, preference feedback, verifiable rewards, and distillation loops.
contextSentence: Internet-scale pretraining remains the foundation of modern LLMs, yet the behavior readers experience is increasingly shaped by later training and feedback loops.
takeaway: Treat broad web-scale pretraining as the base layer, then expect mid-training, instruction tuning, preference optimization, verifiable RL, synthetic traces, and on-policy distillation to refine what the model can do.

conceptualChart.title: Conceptual chart
conceptualChart.body: The stacked areas use default illustrative values bundled at build time. They are meant to communicate direction and relative emphasis, not quantitative market shares of training data.
